summary: Do not ratify as written. The ruling has the right spine, but Case B still permits outcome-aware comparator choice, and the f7 default uses already-seen survivor data inside a future confirmatory slope claim. The pre-spend tool is only a name-level discovery gate, not an RC enforcer. Ratification is safe only after the amendments below make comparator selection data-blind, constrain f7/survivor reuse, and wire first-class reuse blocks into freeze/log/verdict tooling.

loopholes.description: Case-B comparator laundering: RC-4 permits already-unblinded rows as comparator arms but does not require the comparator set/config to be selected by a data-blind rule.
loopholes.exploit_path: After reading f2/f2b logs, choose the weakest known baseline arm/config/seed family as the comparator in a new record, declare all rows matching that config under RC-1, run one fresh arm, and claim a confirmatory win against a baseline whose value and variance were already known. RC-1 prevents row-level cherry-picking after the config is named, but not outcome-driven choice of the config itself.
loopholes.file_ref: docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md §3.3 Case B, RC-1, RC-4; docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md G-10/G-13

loopholes.description: f7 default reuse turns already-seen lower-rung survivor data into part of a future confirmatory slope fit.
loopholes.exploit_path: Let prior verdicts identify survivor arms with favorable R1-R3 behavior, then freeze f7 to reuse those logged R1-R3 cells and run only R4/T*. The primary slope/capstone claim is then partly determined by already-seen selected lower-rung values, not merely by a fresh arm contrasted to a neutral comparator.

loopholes.description: RC-5 overlap re-run is under-specified and can miss arm-specific or backend-specific batch effects.
loopholes.exploit_path: Declare one convenient overlap cell with n>=50 that is known or expected to be stable, pass it, then contrast fresh arms against reused rows from other arms/rungs/producers affected differently by backend, prompt, batching, model-serving, or extraction drift. The miss is treated as environment equality even though the consumed rows were not checked representatively.

loopholes.description: The current kot-reg/1 schema has no lawful place for the RC-1/RC-6 reuse declaration that the prose says is the interim wire format.
loopholes.exploit_path: A consumer record cannot add arbitrary producer row hashes or artifact_hashes under pins because kot-reg/1 pins has additionalProperties:false. Putting them in prose/n_planned/depends_on would not be machine-enforced, so a record can appear frozen while the actual consumed row set, hashes, and selection rule remain outside the freeze machinery.
loopholes.file_ref: docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md §3.3 after RC-6; registry/schema/kot-reg-1.json pins; tools/registry/prereg-freeze.py check_record

loopholes.description: reuse-check.py is a discovery tool, not the binding RC gate described by the ruling.
loopholes.exploit_path: Run the documented record check, get name-level hits only, then rely on a recorded coordinator decision. The tool does not verify exact pins, row hashes, DV computability, instrument gates, RC-4 disclosure, RC-5 overlap, or per-link provenance; it also misses renamed identical arm implementations and flags false positives across different corpora.
loopholes.file_ref: tools/registry/reuse-check.py check/_match; .claude/agents/experiment-runner.md PRE-SPEND REUSE GATE; docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice (5)

loopholes.description: CPU waiver relies on producer-side repeat claims unless tightened to full consumed-output bit recomputation.
loopholes.exploit_path: Claim RC-5 waiver from deterministic_repeat_identical in l3a/a5, but do not recompute and hash the exact consumed outputs under the consumer environment. A stale executable, dependency, or input artifact can pass the paper waiver without proving identity for the rows actually consumed.

per_condition.note: Necessary for freeze declaration and no row/seed cherry-picking. Insufficient because the declared config itself can be chosen after seeing outcomes, and current schema/tooling cannot freeze row hashes in a first-class field.
Exact pin identity is the correct reuse/replication boundary. It must include arm implementation/config hash, not just arm name; the prose says this, the tool does not.
DV computability from logged per-item fields is necessary. It also needs machine checks against the consumer analysis script and instrument-gate fields; current ledger lacks enough enforcement.

Comparator-only plus one fresh arm is not enough. It must also require data-blind comparator family selection, fixed margins/power not conditioned on known comparator values, and special handling for survivor/slope designs such as f7.

Batch-effect measurement is necessary when mixing fresh and reused rows. The overlap cell must be representative or per producer/arm family, and CPU waiver must require full bit-recompute hashes for consumed outputs.

Provenance, no verdict-as-data, and per-link licensing are necessary. It must explicitly ban verdict/outcome-based arm inclusion and require machine-readable reused_from rows consumed by verdict-gen/audit.

Superset logging/raw outputs are essential for future DV computability and audit. Add a hard freeze lint for declared prospective consumers and a default refusal when raw-output persistence is waived without a concrete licence/size rationale.
It is marked load_bearing:false, but f7 reuse accounting is evidentially load-bearing. As written it permits survivor-biased already-seen lower-rung data inside a confirmatory slope fit.

Folding L into one score is directionally good, but registry/components.jsonl consumers are free text and can be gamed. Derived L needs validation against actual pending records/edges, and overrides need audit visibility.

necessity_sufficiency.note: RC-1..RC-6 are mostly individually load-bearing for reuse honesty, but R-2 is a producer/computability rule rather than a pure no-peeking condition, and the ASM entries are not conditions. Joint sufficiency fails because RC-4 does not stop outcome-aware comparator/config selection, f7 uses selected already-seen survivor data inside a primary slope, and the interim wire format is not enforceable under kot-reg/1.

code_vs_policy.note: reuse-check.py check --gate exits 3 on any match, but matching is arm/rung/name-level plus optional simple filters. It does not enforce RC-1 row hashes/selection, RC-2 exact pins, RC-3 DV/instrument transfer, RC-4 comparator-only disclosure, RC-5 overlap, or RC-6 audit traversal. The documented --record invocation in the runner/practices omits --gate, so it reports hits but exits 0; with --gate it is also stronger than prose in false-positive cases because it blocks same-name cells even when corpus/pins differ.

collateral_integrity.note: L-score folding avoids a second prioritisation surface, but the inputs are not yet mechanically trustworthy. f7 accounting is the main integrity failure: it should be load-bearing and selection-adjusted or freshly rerun at lower rungs. A stop-only pre-spend gate before ratification is conservative, but allowing 'consume under RC' decisions before the RC amendments and D9 wiring land is unsafe.

recommended_amendments: Add RC-7 or strengthen RC-4: in Case B, comparator/baseline family and config-selection rule must be fixed by a data-blind rule, a prior frozen mandatory-baseline law, or an exhaustive declared family. If selected after unblinding, the primary is exploratory unless freshly replicated.

Add an explicit f7/survivor rule: already-seen lower-rung data from outcome-selected survivor arms may not enter a confirmatory slope/capstone primary without fresh lower-rung reruns, pre-specified selection-adjusted inference, or permanent exploratory labeling of the slope component.

Tighten RC-5: overlap must cover each reused producer and each material arm/rung/backend family or justify representativeness at freeze; CPU waiver requires recomputing the exact consumed outputs and matching content hashes, not just citing deterministic_repeat_identical.

Land D9 before ratification for any actual consumption: add kot-reg/2 reused_from/artifact_hashes, log-append support for reused rows, verdict-gen eligibility rules, registry-check/prereg-freeze collision refusal, and audit traversal over producer chains.

Change the runner/practices command to use --record ... --gate for paid launches, and extend reuse-check to compare corpus/slice hash, item order, model revision, arm config hash, seeds, decode params, metric fields, row hashes, unblinded status, and renamed identical implementations.

Mark ASM-0010 load_bearing:true and rewrite it as a gate-t5 decision requiring both full-rerun and reuse-adjusted evidential accounting. Validate ASM-0011's derived L against structured registry edges rather than free-text consumers.
reasoning: I read the target plan, the RC ruling and f7/L-score sections, the runner/practices wiring, research-engine §2.7, ASM-0010/0011, the f2b quarantine and f2b-replicate assessment, P2 honesty guardrails G-1/G-13, kot-reg/1 schema, prereg-freeze, and reuse-check.py. I traced reuse-check behavior: record-mode matches only arm/rung names; with --gate it exits 3 on hits, but the documented runner command omits --gate and exits 0. I also checked that kot-reg/1 pins cannot carry arbitrary producer row hashes/artifact hashes, so the stated interim RC-1/RC-6 wire format is not machine-enforced. The decisive adversarial paths are outcome-aware Case-B comparator selection and f7 survivor/slope reuse; both let already-seen data influence a later confirmatory claim while satisfying the current letter of RC-1..RC-6.
